_comment: This file documents every field the Framework Analyzer writes to framework-profile.json. The actual file is written by running the Framework Analyzer agent against your project.
detectedAt: ISO-8601 timestamp when analysis was run
language: java | python | javascript | typescript | csharp | ruby
buildTool: maven | gradle | npm | pip | dotnet | bundler | none
testFramework: One of: cucumber-testng | cucumber-junit | testng | junit5 | junit4 | pytest | behave | robot | jest | playwright | cypress | webdriverio | nunit | mstest | xunit | specflow | rspec-capybara
bdd: true if project uses .feature files (Cucumber/behave/SpecFlow/Robot), false if direct test methods
browserAutomation: selenium-webdriver | playwright | cypress | webdriverio | capybara | robot-seleniumlibrary
assertionLibrary: assertj | testng-assert | junit-assert | hamcrest | jest-expect | chai | pytest-assert | nunit-assert | should-be (Robot)
reportingTool: allure | extent | html-reporter | built-in | junit-xml | none
runCommand: Command to run the full test suite (e.g. 'mvn test', 'pytest tests/', 'npx playwright test')
runSmoke: Command to run smoke tests (e.g. 'mvn test -Psmoke', 'pytest -m smoke')
runSingle: Command to run a single test by tag or name (e.g. 'mvn test -Dcucumber.filter.tags=@{TAG}')
runHeadless: Headless variant of runCommand (e.g. 'SELENIUM_HEADLESS=true mvn test')

structure.testRoot: Absolute or relative path to test source root
structure.stepDefinitions: Path to step definition classes (BDD only)
structure.pageObjects: Path to page object classes (if POM pattern used)
structure.baseClass: Path to the BasePage/BaseTest class (if exists)
structure.testData: Path to test data files (JSON, YAML, CSV, Excel)
structure.reports: Where the framework writes its own reports (e.g. target/allure-results)

locatorStrategy: How selectors are written, e.g. 'By.cssSelector / @FindBy' or 'page.locator()' or 'cy.get()'
tagConvention: How tests are tagged, e.g. '@smoke @regression @P0' or '@pytest.mark.smoke' or '[Category("Smoke")]'

imports._comment: Exact import statements used in this project. Copy from real test files.

codeTemplate: Paste verbatim content of a short, real test file here. This is the most important field — the Jira Test Creator uses this as its style guide.
pageObjectTemplate: Paste verbatim content of a real page object class here (if project uses POM).
stepTemplate: Paste verbatim content of a real step definition file here (BDD projects only).
notes: Any important conventions not captured above
e.g. 'Test data is loaded from tests/fixtures via TestDataReader.get()'
e.g. 'Page objects use @FindBy annotations, not direct By selectors'
